Simultaneously, developing nations argue that carbon taxation disproportionately impacts their economic growth trajectories, necessitating differentiated policy frameworks that balance climate obligations with development imperatives. prompt_inputs.content: Recent advances in CRISPR-Cas9 gene editing have demonstrated remarkable efficacy in treating monogenic disorders through precise DNA sequence modification. The mechanism leverages single-guide RNA (sgRNA) molecules to direct the Cas9 endonuclease to target genomic loci with high specificity, achieving off-target mutation rates below 0.1% in optimized protocols. Furthermore, base editing (BE) and prime editing (PE) represent next-generation approaches that enable A-to-G and C-to-T transitions without double-strand breaks, significantly reducing genotoxic effects. Clinical applications have expanded to include CAR-T cell immunotherapy, where CRISPR modifications enhance T-cell receptor (TCR) specificity and proliferation capacity in hematologic malignancies. However, challenges remain regarding delivery efficiency across the blood-brain barrier (BBB) and mitigating innate immune responses to Cas9 proteins and viral vectors.

solution_criteria: Extracts all major topics including CRISPR-Cas9, gene editing, base editing, prime editing, CAR-T cell immunotherapy, and delivery challenges
Correctly handles technical acronyms (sgRNA, BE, PE, TCR, BBB) by incorporating them into or associating them with appropriate topic names
Returns output as a JSON array of strings with no additional formatting or metadata

reasoning: The solution fully satisfies all mandatory requirements: it provides a JSON array of strings containing topics from the article, with no extra commentary, and returns only the JSON array. The solution appropriately prioritizes the most significant concepts discussed in the passage.
